{{< pagebreak >}}

# Day 2 — Pause before one automatic purchase {#day2}

```{=typst}
#wcg-lead[Yesterday you noticed a pattern. Today you add one small thing: a pause.]
#wcg-lesson-art("pause", caption: [A short, quiet moment before one automatic choice])
```

## Why this matters

Many purchases feel finished before we notice deciding. Research on unplanned
buying suggests that situational factors — time to browse, and how we feel in the
moment — often come *before* the purchase itself.^5^

People also differ in how much discomfort they feel when spending, sometimes
called the "pain of paying."^6^ A short pause simply gives that natural signal a
moment to be heard.

## An everyday example

Picture yourself at night, tired, thumb hovering over "Buy now." A ten-second
pause does not cancel the purchase. It just opens a small window where a real
choice can happen.

```{=typst}
#wcg-action[
  Choose one moment where buying tends to happen fast. Before that purchase
  today, take about ten quiet seconds. Notice how you feel. Then decide — buy it
  or not. Either answer is fine.
]
```

> **Notice.**
>
> What am I feeling right now — and do I still want this in a minute?

A pause is a small act of respect for yourself. It is not a rule to obey.

{{< include assets/worksheets/ws-2-purchase-pause.qmd >}}
